title: Expressing the sense of the House of Representatives that the Parthenon Marbles should be returned to Greece.
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.
plain_summary: This resolution says that the US House of Representatives thinks the United Kingdom should talk with Greece about giving back ancient marble sculptures called the Parthenon Marbles. These sculptures were taken from Greece more than 200 years ago when another country controlled Greece. The sculptures are now in a museum in London, but they originally came from an old Greek temple called the Parthenon in Athens. The House believes these important pieces of Greek history should go back to Greece where they belong.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution expresses the opinion of the House of Representatives that the Parthenon Marbles should be returned to Greece.

sections.title: Background Facts
sections.text: The Parthenon was built on the Acropolis hill in Athens, Greece, in the middle of the fifth century before Christ. It was built under the direction of an Athenian leader named Pericles and designed by a sculptor named Phidias. The Parthenon represents the highest achievement of Greek artistic genius and is the most important symbol of Greek culture, including their art, building styles, and democracy. It shows the contributions that modern Greeks and their ancestors have made to civilization. The Parthenon has been used as a place of worship by ancient Greeks, Christians, and Muslims. The United States has copied the Parthenon's design in many important public buildings, including the Lincoln Memorial. More than one hundred pieces of the Parthenon's sculptures, now called the Parthenon Marbles, were removed from the Parthenon under questionable circumstances between eighteen oh one and eighteen sixteen. They were taken by a British nobleman named Thomas Bruce, who was the seventh Earl of Elgin, while Greece was still controlled by the Ottoman Empire. The removal of the Parthenon Marbles, including their dangerous trip to Great Britain and careless storage there for many years, put the Marbles in great danger. The Parthenon Marbles were removed to decorate Lord Elgin's private home. He only gave the Marbles to the British Museum after he faced serious money problems. The sculptures of the Parthenon were designed as a complete part of the Parthenon temple structure. The carvings on the friezes, pediments, and metopes are not just statues or movable decorative art, but are built-in parts of the Parthenon that can best be appreciated if all the Parthenon Marbles are brought back together. The Parthenon is a worldwide symbol of culture, democracy, and freedom, making the Parthenon Marbles important not only to Greece but to all the world. Since gaining independence in eighteen thirty, Greece has asked for the return of the Parthenon Marbles. Returning the Parthenon Marbles would be a meaningful way for the United Kingdom to show its appreciation and respect for the Parthenon and classical art. Returning the Parthenon Marbles to Greece would be a gesture of good will by the British Parliament and would not set any legal precedent or affect the ownership of other objects in museums in the United States or around the world. The United Kingdom should return the Parthenon Marbles because the Parthenon is part of the cultural heritage of the entire world and should be made complete. Greece would provide care for the Parthenon Marbles that is equal or better than the care provided by the British Museum, especially considering the permanent damage caused by the museum's attempts to remove the original color and surface coating of the Marbles with harsh cleaning chemicals. Greece opened the New Acropolis Museum in two thousand nine, which houses the Marbles that were not removed to the United Kingdom by Lord Elgin. The museum displays them alongside plaster copies of the Marbles currently in the British Museum so visitors can see the entire frieze as it was designed around the Temple of Athena. It has been two hundred years since the British Government voted to buy the Marbles. The Greek Government has publicly supported working with the British Government in a diplomatic way to negotiate mutually agreeable conditions for the return of the Parthenon Marbles. The people of Greece have a greater, ancient connection to the Parthenon Marbles, which were in Greece for over two thousand two hundred years of the over two thousand four hundred thirty year history of the Parthenon. British Members of Parliament from across the political spectrum have supported legislation to authorize the return of the Marbles to Greece.

sections.title: Resolution
sections.text: Therefore, it is the opinion of the House of Representatives that the Government of the United Kingdom should enter into negotiations with the Government of Greece to help return the Parthenon Marbles to Greece.
